Installing VS Code & How Websites Work:
### Installing VS Code & How Websites Work

- **Visual Studio Code (VS Code) Installation**:
  - **Download**: Visit the official website to download VS Code for your OS.
  - **Installation**: Follow the installation prompts specific to your platform (Windows, macOS, Linux).
  - **Extensions**: Install useful extensions like HTML, CSS, JavaScript support for enhanced functionality.

- **Understanding How Websites Work**:
  - **Frontend vs. Backend**:
    - **Frontend**: The client-side; what users see (HTML, CSS, JavaScript).
    - **Backend**: The server-side; handles data processing and storage (Node.js, databases).
  - **HTTP/HTTPS Protocol**: Communication between the client and server.
  - **Web Browsers**: Render websites using the code sent from the server.
  - **Web Hosting**: Services that store and serve your website files to users.

These concepts provide a foundational understanding of web development and the tools involved.
Basic Structure of an HTML Website: - **HTML**: Stands for **HyperText Markup Language**, foundational for web content. - **Document Structure**: - Begins with `` declaration. - Root element: `` tag. - **Head Section**: - Contains metadata within `` tags. - Includes ``, links to stylesheets, and scripts. - **Body Section**: - Main content area enclosed in `<body>` tags. - Contains visible elements like headings, paragraphs, images, and links. - **Common Tags**: - **Headings**: `<h1>` to `<h6>` for titles and subtitles. - **Paragraphs**: `<p>` for text. - **Links**: `<a href="">` for hyperlinks. - **Images**: `<img src="" alt="">` for images. - **Semantic HTML**: Use of tags that convey meaning (e.g., `<header>`, `<footer>`, `<article>`). - **Closing Tags**: Most elements require closing tags for proper structure.
Your First HTML Website: - **HTML Basics**: Understand the structure of HTML documents, including tags and elements. - **Essential Tags**: - **``**: Root element of an HTML page. - **``**: Contains meta-information about the document. - **``**: Sets the title of the webpage. - **`<body>`**: Main content area of the webpage. - **Text Formatting Tags**: - **`<h1>` to `<h6>`**: Header tags for titles and subtitles. - **`<p>`**: Defines a paragraph. - **`<a>`**: Hyperlink tag for linking to other pages. - **Images and Media**: - **`<img>`**: Embeds images; requires `src` and `alt` attributes. - **Lists**: - **`<ul>`**: Unordered list. - **`<ol>`**: Ordered list. - **`<li>`**: List item. - **Saving and Opening**: Save files with a **.html** extension and open in a web browser to view.
